feat: add existing-repo analysis protocol to githubKit Discover phase (#110)

* chore: log #17 implementation to bender history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(security): add path/ref validation and rate-limit guards (#17)

Issue 1 — Input validation:
- New github-input-validation.ts module with validatePath() and validateRef()
- Rejects path traversal (../), absolute paths, null bytes, control chars
- Validates git refs against git-check-ref-format rules (SHA, branch, tag)
- Wired into github_repo_tree and github_repo_file_read tools

Issue 2 — Rate-limit guard:
- New github-rate-limit.ts singleton tracker reads X-RateLimit-* headers
- GitHubConnector.request() overridden to enforce pre-flight check and
  update tracker from every response
- Tools surface rateLimitWarning in responses when remaining is low
- Blocks requests (RATE_LIMITED error) when quota is exhausted

Includes 32 new tests covering all validation rules and rate-limit states.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.3.20: transfer stock during pack conversion in optimizer

When optimizer merges a multi-pack product into its base product, it now
moves all stock entries to the base product with amount × pack_size before
deleting the multi-pack. Previously, DELETE FROM products cascaded into the
stock table, silently dropping all existing stock.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: atomic webhook claim + credit row-lock to eliminate race conditions

- billing_service: replace is_event_processed+mark_event (check-then-insert,
  not race-safe) with claim_webhook_event (INSERT, catch IntegrityError) +
  update_webhook_status (UPDATE only). Two concurrent Stripe retries for the
  same event_id can no longer double-process: DB UNIQUE constraint on
  stripe_event_id is the synchronization gate.

- billing router: use claim_webhook_event/update_webhook_status. Legacy
  is_event_processed/mark_event kept as deprecated aliases.

- credit_service.add_credits: SELECT user with with_for_update() to prevent
  concurrent credit additions from reading stale balance.

- credit_service.deduct_credits: re-fetch user with with_for_update() inside
  the deduction transaction instead of trusting the potentially-stale caller-
  provided user object. Prevents concurrent AI requests from both passing the
  balance check and double-deducting.

- tests/test_credits.py: update mocks to reflect the re-fetch pattern
  (side_effect list instead of single return_value).

All 12 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(direct): implement direct connection mode (no relay) (#19)

* feat(direct): implement direct connection mode (no relay)

Add direct connection mode allowing Yandex to call MA server directly
via HTTPS without the yaha-cloud.ru WebSocket relay.

New files:
- provider/direct.py: DirectConnectionHandler with HTTP routes for
  Smart Home API endpoints and OAuth account linking
- tests/test_direct.py: 36 tests covering auth, health, API, OAuth,
  and plugin integration

Modified:
- provider/plugin.py: Add _start_direct_mode() and cleanup in unload()
- provider/__init__.py: Add direct mode config entries with endpoint
  URLs, OAuth URLs, skill_id/skill_token fields
- provider/constants.py: Add direct-mode constants (paths, OAuth, etc.)
- README.md, CLAUDE.md: Document direct connection mode

The handler registers dynamic routes on MA's built-in webserver using
mass.webserver.register_dynamic_route(). OAuth implements minimal
Authorization Code flow for Yandex account linking.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(direct): address Copilot review — XSS, OAuth validation, URL normalization

- HTML-escape redirect_url in OAuth authorize page (XSS fix)
- Validate client_id, response_type, redirect_uri domain in /auth/authorize
- Validate client_id and client_secret in /auth/token endpoint
- Replace hardcoded DIRECT_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET with per-install generated secret
- Add CONF_DIRECT_CLIENT_SECRET to config entries with auto-generation
- Normalize base_url with rstrip('/') to prevent double slashes
- Preserve CONF_DIRECT_ACCESS_TOKEN value on config save
- Restructure README install steps (Cloud vs Direct sections)
- Add 5 new tests for validation (41 direct tests, 157 total)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: bump version to 1.1.0, update CHANGELOG

fix: remove unsupported upper() from provision workflow

GitHub Actions expressions support format() but not upper(), so the
workflow failed to parse before any job could start.

Use a supported conditional expression to select the PROD/TEST secret
and variable names, then reuse those values via job-level env vars.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Fix appsettings.json RagPipeline BaseUrl: railway internal port 5000 → 8080

  - Section 3: point to mtg-forge-card-ai repo, correct env var names and port

Add all project files: specs, assets, build scripts, design system

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: raise MAX_MAGLEV_DEOPT_RETRIES from 10 to 1000

10 deopts permanently disabled Maglev. During Criterion's 1-second
warmup, cold JIT ICs can easily trigger 10+ deopts, killing Maglev
for the entire measurement phase. With 1000 retries, exponential
backoff (capped at 1024 invocations) handles transient deopts while
still allowing Maglev to stabilize.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: move env/pkgs/registry setup into grazhda.sh, remove zgard init

1. Move .grazhda.env from $HOME to $GRAZHDA_DIR — all source code
   now resolves env file path within the Grazhda installation directory.

2. Copy .grazhda.pkgs.yaml into $GRAZHDA_DIR during both fresh install
   (grazhda.sh) and upgrade (grazhda upgrade).

3. Add version field to PackageDef in the registry schema — each package
   definition can now carry a default version.

4. Remove zgard init command — all infrastructure setup (mkdir pkgs,
   create .grazhda.env, copy registry, patch init script) now happens
   automatically during grazhda.sh installation and grazhda upgrade.

5. $GRAZHDA_DIR/pkgs directory is always created during installation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Restrict Biome Includes to Supported File Types (#179)

Biome's `files.includes: ["**"]` caused biome to discover and parse
markdown files, triggering a panic in biome_markdown_parser on Chinese
text (byte boundary error). Restrict includes to only the file types
biome should actually process (JSON, JS/TS, CSS).

commit.message:
v0.3.19: preserve recipe-linked group-masters during optimizer parent cleanup

_strip_parents now checks recipe_ingredients before deleting group-master
products. Parents that are referenced by a recipe ingredient are kept in
the DB (but still deactivated + excluded from AI) so recipe links survive
the optimization run. Only orphan group-masters with no recipe references
are deleted.

during install, breaking both lint and test jobs.

commit.message:
campaign: bump target F1 0.95→0.99, add SWA support, fix arch key bug + state reconciliation

Key changes:
- ml_metaopt_campaign.yaml: target_metric updated to 0.99 (from 0.95)
  - Updated comment to reflect measured PP ceiling of 0.9888
- scripts/train.py: add optional SWA phase (swa_start_epoch/swa_epochs/swa_lr config)
  - Activates post-convergence SWA averaging when configured
  - Reverts to pre-SWA best if SWA model is worse by >0.02

Critical bug fixed (in prior session): model.py config key 'arch' (not 'conv_type')
was causing all TransformerConv runs to silently train GATConv. Fixing jumped F1
from 0.86 → 0.9792.

Architecture ceiling confirmed: TransformerConv 5L/128h/4heads (3.44M params)
converges to F1=0.977-0.979 regardless of model size (3M-22M), loss, or LR.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix MICROSOFT_TENANT_ID being overwritten by login tenant

The auto-discover logic was overwriting the user-provided
MICROSOFT_TENANT_ID with the Azure login tenant ID. Now it only
auto-discovers when MICROSOFT_TENANT_ID is not set in .env, so the
Step 4 tenant comparison correctly detects the mismatch and switches
to the M365 tenant before creating the app registration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(search): Phase 2 — multi-engine fan-out with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (#22)

* feat: implement Phase 3 e-commerce heuristics and related product pruning

* docs: modernize OUTPUT_CONTRACT and EXTRACTION wiki references

* feat(core): modernize extraction engine, eradicate panics, and add cloud jina opt-in

This commit resolves major architectural goals by hardening execution stability,
improving text-extraction heuristics, and enforcing privacy-by-default routing:

1. Panic Eradication & Error Handling
   - System-wide removal of `unwrap()` and `expect()`, replacing them with safe
     `Result` propagation.
   - Refactored `RipwebError` and domain errors (`DdgError`, `GithubError`, etc.)
     to leverage the `thiserror` crate.
   - Updated `main()` to return `anyhow::Result<()>`, migrating `std::process::exit()`
     calls to graceful `anyhow::bail!` error bubbling.

2. AST-Based Semantic Pruning
   - Replaced fragile character-counting link saturation heuristics with mathematical
     AST calculations via the `tl` crate (`evaluate_block_saturation`).
   - Accurately computes link density by evaluating structural DOM nodes,
     significantly improving the accuracy of sidebar and navigation pruning.

3. Privacy by Default
   - Added an explicit `--allow-cloud` CLI flag to gate third-party
     extraction proxies (Jina.ai).
   - Forces `ripweb` to strictly adhere to localized parsing and heuristic fallbacks
     unless explicitly given permission to use offsite JS rendering engines.

Also updates `CURRENT_PRIORITIES.md` to mark these milestones as completed.

* fix(ci): repair PR #7 branch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(mode): add 7-variant Mode enum per output contract

* feat(cli): replace --verbosity u8 with --mode Mode

* fix(cli): update verbosity_default test to use Mode::Balanced

* feat(run): thread Mode through format functions, add source delimiters

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(search): thread Mode through all platform format functions

Replace verbosity: u8 with mode: crate::mode::Mode in all 6 search
platform files, dispatching via mode.density_tier(). Update inline
tests to pass Mode variants instead of raw integers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(normalize): strip UTM and tracking params from cache keys per output contract

* feat(output): emit mode/token/results header per output contract

* test(output_contract): rewrite tests against Mode enum, add delimiter/tracking coverage

Also fixes apostle_extraction.rs which had the same u8 verbosity → Mode type errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(e2e): update cli_e2e tests for --mode flag and new output format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(eval): add BenchmarkQuery and SearchResultRecord types

* feat(eval): add QueryTrace type for per-query execution audit

* feat(eval): add regression and techdocs benchmark fixtures

* feat(eval): add JSONL fixture loader in tests/common

* feat(eval): add EvalMetrics, success_at_k, MRR, nDCG@10 metric computation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(eval): add snapshot eval harness for baseline regression and techdocs metrics

* feat(eval): add eval-search and eval-search-regression just targets

* style: apply cargo fmt across all files

* feat(scoring): add ScorerInput/ScoredResult types and refactor QueryTrace scorer_contributions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(config): add SearchConfig with trust tiers, blocklist, and scoring weights

* feat(scoring): add domain_trust and blocklist_penalty scorers

* feat(scoring): add url_pattern scorer

* feat(scoring): add project_match and snippet_relevance scorers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(scoring): add domain_diversity scorer

* feat(scoring): add score_results pipeline wiring all six scorers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(pipeline): wire score_results into handle_query; add phase1 scored eval snapshots

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(fusion): add rrf_fuse with URL normalisation and dedup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(search): add FanOut engine variant and fan_out_search with RRF

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(search): stub FanOut arm in search_query with explicit error

* feat(run): wire FanOut engine into handle_query with fan_out_search branching

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(eval): extend BenchmarkQuery with supplemental_results; add fan-out fixtures

Adds `supplemental_results: HashMap<String, Vec<SearchResultRecord>>` field
(serde default = empty map) to `BenchmarkQuery` for multi-engine fan-out eval.
Creates 10-query fixture set across regression_fanout.jsonl and techdocs_fanout.jsonl.

* test(eval): add Phase 2 fan-out eval tests with non-regression assertions

* style: apply cargo fmt to Phase 1 scoring files

Co-authored-by: Ivy <ivy@example.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Sessions: filter out Claude customization directories (#309105)

* Filter out Claude customization directories in sessions app

The sessions (Agents) window was incorrectly showing and allowing
creation of customizations in .claude directories. Add protected
hook methods getPromptSourceFolders() and getDefaultSourceFolders()
to PromptFilesLocator, then override them in AgenticPromptFilesLocator
to filter out Claude-specific sources (ClaudePersonal, ClaudeWorkspace,
ClaudeWorkspaceLocal).

This ensures the sessions app only shows .github and .copilot
customization directories.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: path-based Claude filtering and fix JSDoc

- Also filter user-configured .claude paths (Config* source types)
  by checking the path string, not just the PromptFileSource enum
- Fix inaccurate JSDoc comments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v1.5.12: recipe stubs created as inactive group-master parent products

Unmatched recipe ingredients now get stub products that are:
- active=False (won't appear in stock overview)
- product_group_id = 'Group master' (same as optimizer-created parents)
- min_stock_amount=0

The 'Group master' product group is looked up first and created if missing.
This ensures the optimizer's _strip_parents correctly identifies and deletes
stub parents before the AI runs, saving tokens and preventing duplication.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
patch 9.2.0334: GTK: window geometry shrinks with with client-side decorations

Problem:  On GTK3 with client-side decorations the window opens with
          wrong &columns/&lines, and each :tabnew/:tabclose cycle
          shrinks the size further.
Solution: Measure and compensate for the CSD frame offset, discard
          spurious configure events from tabline show/hide
          (Gary Johnson).

closes: #19853

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Gary Johnson <garyjohn@spocom.com>
Signed-off-by: Christian Brabandt <cb@256bit.org>

commit.message:
perf: reinitialize-in-place object creation + bypass RefCell borrows

Three optimizations for object_creation_1k:
1. Reinitialize-in-place: when target register holds sole-owner
   PlainObject from same template, overwrite values via memcpy
   without touching the Rc pool (eliminates 2 TLS accesses/iter)
2. Bypass RefCell borrow on template cache: unchecked path for
   hot fused CreateObjectLiteral handlers
3. Recycle old objects in dispatch fused path via swap+recycle

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: modernize OUTPUT_CONTRACT and EXTRACTION wiki references

* feat(core): modernize extraction engine, eradicate panics, and add cloud jina opt-in

This commit resolves major architectural goals by hardening execution stability,
improving text-extraction heuristics, and enforcing privacy-by-default routing:

1. Panic Eradication & Error Handling
   - System-wide removal of `unwrap()` and `expect()`, replacing them with safe
     `Result` propagation.
   - Refactored `RipwebError` and domain errors (`DdgError`, `GithubError`, etc.)
     to leverage the `thiserror` crate.
   - Updated `main()` to return `anyhow::Result<()>`, migrating `std::process::exit()`
     calls to graceful `anyhow::bail!` error bubbling.

2. AST-Based Semantic Pruning
   - Replaced fragile character-counting link saturation heuristics with mathematical
     AST calculations via the `tl` crate (`evaluate_block_saturation`).
   - Accurately computes link density by evaluating structural DOM nodes,
     significantly improving the accuracy of sidebar and navigation pruning.

3. Privacy by Default
   - Added an explicit `--allow-cloud` CLI flag to gate third-party
     extraction proxies (Jina.ai).
   - Forces `ripweb` to strictly adhere to localized parsing and heuristic fallbacks
     unless explicitly given permission to use offsite JS rendering engines.

Also updates `CURRENT_PRIORITIES.md` to mark these milestones as completed.

* fix(ci): repair PR #7 branch

fix(conv): chunk _forward_normalized and stage fitting to prevent OOM

_forward_normalized unfolds all N images into a (N, L, patch_dim)
tensor which exceeds GPU memory for large batches (e.g. 10k images
with patch_size=7: 5.6 GB). Process in chunks capped at ~1 GB of
unfolded data.

Also chunk the sequential stage fitting loop (stage(x_sub)) which
passes all subsample images through a fitted stage at once.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix vec_chunks dimension mismatch on embedding model switch

ensureVecTable() used CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, which is a
no-op when the table already exists. When the embedding model changes
dimensions (e.g. nomic 768d → Qwen3-Embedding 1024d), the vec0 virtual
table retains its old dimension specification. Every subsequent INSERT
into vec_chunks fails with a dimension mismatch, breaking enrichment
and vector search for all repos.

Fix: probe the existing vec_chunks table's actual dimension by reading
a sample embedding blob. If the dimensions don't match the current
provider, DROP and recreate the table, then resync compatible embeddings
from the blob-based embeddings table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix hero section mobile stacking - proper flex ordering

Hero section was not stacking correctly on mobile because order property
was applied to nested children instead of flex container direct children.

Changes:
- Reordered flex children so mascot (hero-right) appears first on mobile (order: 1)
- Text overlay (hero-left) appears second (order: 2)
- Uses proper flex-direction: column on mobile, switches to grid on desktop
- Removed nested order properties; now uses top-level order on direct children
- Mobile: full-width mascot section first, then text + image below
- Tablet: horizontal layout with proper left/right split
- Desktop: restored classic 60/40 grid with absolute positioning overlays

All breakpoints tested:
- Mobile (< 768px): Single column, mascot first
- Tablet (768px+): Two-column flexible layout
- Desktop (1024px+): Grid layout with background image positioning

Build: ✅ Passes
Lint: ✅ Passes

fix: remove start-recording toast notifications (#69)

* fix: remove start-recording toast notifications

Both 'Echo recording started' and 'GIF recording started' info toasts
are visible in recordings/GIFs. Status bar already reflects state —
no toast needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: increase timeout on platform helper tests to avoid CI flake on Windows

The execFile call in getWindowInfo() has a 10 s internal timeout.
Mocha's default 2 s fired first on Windows CI, causing a false failure.
Set per-test timeout to 15 s for all async getWindowScaleFactor tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
